items.q: Which verb is used to say 'I am happy today' (temporary state)?
items.why: Students treat ser and estar as identical. SER for permanent traits, identity, time, origin. ESTAR for temporary states, location, ongoing actions. 'Soy contento' would suggest 'I am a happy person', not 'I'm happy right now'.

items.q: What is the 'nosotros' form of 'hablar' (to speak)?
items.why: Students confuse nosotros (we) with ellos (they). AR verbs in present: hablo, hablas, habla, HABLAMOS, habláis, hablan. The nosotros ending is -amos, not -an.

items.q: When do you use the imperfect rather than the preterite?
items.correct: For ongoing, habitual, or descriptive past actions.
items.misconception: When the action happened a long time ago.
items.random_wrong: When you're unsure of the exact date.
items.why: Students choose tense by recency. The distinction is ASPECTUAL: preterite = completed/single ('comí' I ate); imperfect = ongoing/habitual ('comía' I used to eat / I was eating).

items.q: Which sentence correctly says 'I get on well with my sister'?
items.correct: Me llevo bien con mi hermana.
items.misconception: Me llevo bueno con mi hermana.
items.random_wrong: Yo llevo bien con mi hermana.
items.why: It's Me llevo bien con mi hermana.. “Me llevo bueno con mi hermana.” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Which is the correct use of ser/estar: 'I am tired'?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Estoy cansado.. “Soy cansado.” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: How many forms does the Spanish adjective 'simpático' have (masc sing, masc plur, fem sing, fem plur)?
items.why: The real figure is 4 forms. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
